summary: HEAD 704b636a1 equals origin/main, and no recent commit references #3261. The generic authenticated container-job backend and documentation exist, but the issue-specific managed-session cutover is absent: managed sessions still create and clean up DinD sidecars, inject session-local DOCKER_HOST, expose Docker rather than containerJobs capability metadata, and run Docker CLI sidecar preflight. Repository searches found no managed-session client/tool adapter, scoped authenticated channel, containerJobs runtime descriptor, managed-session job correlation/evidence integration, or public-service cancellation policy. All ten acceptance criteria therefore require implementation.

requirements.description: A managed session can discover and call all five canonical container-job tools through an authenticated MoonMind channel.
Submission uses an authorized managed-session workspace locator and returns a distinct stable job ID immediately.
The runtime capability advertises `containerJobs` and does not advertise/inject session-local `DOCKER_HOST`.
Managed agents can poll/follow bounded status/logs and retrieve durable artifact references without holding a single call open for job duration.
Job records correlate to session/turn/thread/epoch where available while remaining separate from session and `MoonMind.AgentRun` identity.
Session observability can link to job evidence without treating job events as session lifecycle truth.
A turn ending does not kill the job container; session termination only affects the job through an explicit authorized cancellation policy.
No managed-session container/image receives the system Docker proxy, Docker socket, raw host path, or registry credentials.
Default managed-session configuration no longer depends on a DinD sidecar or Docker CLI for repository container jobs.
Sidecar/direct-Docker capability can be disabled before physical removal without breaking supported container-job use.
